title: Analog Space Research Stations and Tso Kar Valley
focus: Objectives of analog space research, global stations, and the selection of Tso Kar Valley for Mars-like conditions.
rationale: This fragment covers the specific concept of analog space research, its objectives, and the geographical and scientific significance of Tso Kar Valley.
sectionExcerpt: Led by: ISRO’s Human Space Flight Centre (HSFC)... Simulates extraterrestrial environments like the Moon and Mars. Enables testing of human survival, health protocols, and equipment... Other similar research stations across the World: Mars Desert Research Station of the US, Flashline Mars Arctic Station of Canada, and BIOS-3 of Russia. Why Tso Kar Valley was chosen? Mars-like conditions: High UV radiation, Low atmospheric pressure, Extreme cold and Saline permafrost.
evidenceBullets: Analog space research is led by ISRO's Human Space Flight Centre (HSFC) to simulate extraterrestrial environments.
Global analog stations include the Mars Desert Research Station (US), Flashline Mars Arctic Station (Canada), and BIOS-3 (Russia).
Tso Kar Valley was chosen for its Mars-like conditions, which include high UV radiation, low atmospheric pressure, extreme cold, and saline permafrost.

title: ISRO's Launch Vehicles: LMLV, PSLV, GSLV, LVM3, and SSLV
focus: Features and capabilities of ISRO's launch vehicles, including the upcoming LMLV and recent PSLV missions.
rationale: Provides a comprehensive overview of ISRO's launch infrastructure, detailing the stages, payload capacities, and specific missions associated with each vehicle type.
sectionExcerpt: ISRO’s heaviest rocket Lunar Module Launch Vehicle (LMLV) will be ready by 2035... 3 Stages: Liquid propellant for its first two stages and a cryogenic propellant for its third stage. PSLV: ISRO's workhorse... a four-stage vehicle and first Indian launch vehicle to be equipped with liquid stages. GSLV: A fourth-generation, three-stage vehicle designed to launch 2.0-ton class satellites into GTO. LVM 3: A heavy-lift, three-stage vehicle capable of carrying 4-ton class satellites to GTO or about 10 tons to LEO. SSLV: Three-stage, all-solid propulsion vehicle designed for launching Mini, Micro, or Nano satellites. PSLV-C61/EOS-09 Mission failed due to a technical malfunction during the third stage.
evidenceBullets: The Lunar Module Launch Vehicle (LMLV), ready by 2035, is a 3-stage vehicle utilizing liquid propellant for the first two stages and cryogenic for the third.
PSLV is a four-stage vehicle and the first Indian launch vehicle equipped with liquid stages.
LVM 3 is a heavy-lift three-stage vehicle capable of carrying 4-ton class satellites to GTO or 10 tons to LEO.
SSLV is a three-stage, all-solid propulsion vehicle designed specifically for Mini, Micro, or Nano satellites.
EOS-09 is an advanced Earth observation satellite equipped with C-band SAR technology.

title: Satellite Internet Services and Global Constellations
focus: Mechanics, infrastructure, and prominent global projects of satellite internet.
rationale: Covers the distinct technology of satellite broadband, its infrastructure components, latency issues, and major global constellations.
sectionExcerpt: A wireless internet connection provided through communication satellites orbiting the Earth. Unlike land-based internet services... it doesn’t rely on wires to transmit data. Infrastructure: Generally includes three segments: Space Segment: It is mainly a constellation system... Ground segment: Includes satellite measurement and control networks, gateway stations, etc. Latency: Satellite internet typically has higher latency compared to traditional wired connections due to the distance data must travel. Various prominent Satellite Internet projects: Project Kuiper: by Amazon, Starlink: Launched by SpaceX, OneWeb: By French satellite operator Eutelsat, Qianfan constellation: Planned Chinese low-Earth orbit satellite internet megaconstellation.
evidenceBullets: Satellite internet infrastructure comprises a Space Segment (satellite constellations) and a Ground Segment (gateway stations and control networks).
Satellite internet typically experiences higher latency than traditional wired connections due to the distance data must travel to and from the satellite.
Prominent global satellite internet projects include Project Kuiper (Amazon), Starlink (SpaceX), OneWeb (Eutelsat), and the Qianfan constellation (China).

title: Space Phenomena: Solar Flares and Coronal Mass Ejections (CMEs)
focus: Characteristics, classification, and effects of Solar Flares and CMEs.
rationale: Focuses on solar weather phenomena, specifically the definitions, classifications, and occurrences of solar flares and CMEs, which is a distinct scientific topic.
sectionExcerpt: Solar flares are giant explosions on the sun that send energy, light and high speed particles into space. Classification: The biggest flares are known as "X-class flares". The smallest ones are A-class (near background levels), followed by B, C, M and X. Frequency: Flares occur more frequently during the solar maximum (peak of the 11-year solar cycle). Coronal Mass Ejections (CMEs): They are large expulsions of plasma and magnetic field from the Sun’s corona. Recently, Chandrayaan-2 Lunar Orbiter (CHACE-2) has made first-ever observation of effects of sun’s Coronal Mass Ejection (CME) on moon.
evidenceBullets: Solar flares are classified from smallest to largest as A, B, C, M, and X-class flares.
Solar flares occur most frequently during the solar maximum, which is the peak of the Sun's 11-year magnetic cycle.
Coronal Mass Ejections (CMEs) are large expulsions of plasma and magnetic fields from the Sun's corona.
Chandrayaan-2's CHACE-2 made the first-ever observation of the effects of a Coronal Mass Ejection on the Moon.
